scope.summary:
Implement the top project-tabs bar — a horizontal strip of clickable tabs, one per Handshake workspace (project), rendered at the top of the shell above the pane grid. Switching a project tab restores that project's saved pane layout (not the files/documents inside the panes — those are pane-local state). This is distinct from pane-local tabs (MT-013) and the module switcher (MT-012).

Goal and expected diff shape: create src/frontend/navigation/project_tabs.rs exporting a ProjectTabBar widget; wire it into src/frontend/shell.rs App struct so it sits between the header bar and the pane grid; connect it to the WorkbenchLayoutStore introduced by MT-006 (layout persistence) to trigger project-switch layout restore.

Exact files to create/edit:
  - CREATE: src/frontend/navigation/project_tabs.rs
  - CREATE: src/frontend/navigation/mod.rs (if it does not exist yet; otherwise add pub mod project_tabs)
  - EDIT: src/frontend/shell.rs — call ProjectTabBar::show() in the main egui top panel, forward project_switched callbacks to the layout store
  - EDIT: src/frontend/state/app_state.rs (or equivalent AppState struct from MT-005) — add active_project_id: String field and projects: Vec<ProjectItem> field

Data types (mirror the React originals, name them identically in Rust):
  - ProjectItem { id: String, name: String }
  - active_project_id: String (matches the activeProjectId state in App.tsx line 574)

Backend binding — on startup and on refresh, call the REST endpoint GET /workspaces (api.ts line 733: listWorkspaces -> GET http://127.0.0.1:37501/workspaces) which returns Vec<Workspace> where Workspace has { id, name, created_at, updated_at }. Map each to ProjectItem { id, name }. In native Rust use the existing handshake_core HTTP client or reqwest (already in Cargo if present) for this call. Do NOT rebuild any backend logic.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. In src/frontend/navigation/project_tabs.rs define: struct ProjectTabBar { projects: Vec<ProjectItem>, active_id: String } with a fn show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui) -> Option<String> that returns Some(project_id) when the user clicks a different tab.
  2. Render using ui.horizontal(|ui| { for project in &self.projects { let active = project.id == self.active_id; if ui.selectable_label(active, &project.name).clicked() { result = Some(project.id.clone()); } } }).
  3. Every tab button MUST receive an AccessKit author_id via egui's sense/response layer: call response.widget_info(egui::WidgetInfo::selected(egui::WidgetType::Button, ui.is_enabled(), active, &project.name)) and set a stable id using ui.push_id(&project.id, ...) so AccessKit picks it up as a stable node. The AccessKit role is Button with Toggle action.
  4. In shell.rs App::update(): call project_tabs.show(ui) inside egui::TopBottomPanel::top("project-tabs"). If a new project_id is returned, call app_state.switch_project(new_id) which: (a) stores active_project_id, (b) saves the current pane layout for the old project via WorkbenchLayoutStore::save(old_id, layout) (reuse MT-006 store), (c) loads the stored layout for the new project via WorkbenchLayoutStore::load(new_id) and applies it, falling back to default layout if absent.
  5. On first load fetch workspaces from GET /workspaces; if the list is empty show a single placeholder tab 'No projects'. On error keep the previous list.
  6. Add a refresh trigger: a channel or flag that the WorkspaceSidebar (future MT) can send to re-fetch the workspace list (matches the refreshKey integer in App.tsx line 573).

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-005 (typed pane registry / AppState skeleton) must exist so app_state.rs fields can be added.
  - MT-006 (layout persistence / WorkbenchLayoutStore) must exist so project-switch can save+restore layouts.
  - Neither MT-007 (pop-out) nor MT-008+ are required by this MT.

Risk if missed: no project isolation; switching context loses or corrupts pane layout; agents cannot navigate between projects by stable id.
acceptance_criteria: A horizontal project-tab strip renders at the top of the shell window above the pane grid with one tab per workspace returned by GET /workspaces.

Clicking a project tab that is not active switches active_project_id, saves the current layout for the previous project, and restores the stored layout for the new project (or default layout if none stored).
Switching back to the previous project restores the layout that was saved when leaving it.
The active tab is visually distinguished (egui selectable_label active=true or equivalent theming).
Every project tab button has a stable AccessKit author_id derived from project.id, role=Button, and a Toggle/Click action detectable by an accessibility consumer.
When GET /workspaces returns an empty list, a single disabled 'No projects' tab is shown without crashing.
When the backend is unreachable at startup, the previous workspace list is retained and an inline error label is shown.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_frontend -- navigation::project_tabs — unit test: given projects=[{id:'a',name:'Alpha'},{id:'b',name:'Beta'}] and active_id='a', clicking 'Beta' returns Some('b').

cargo test -p handshake_frontend -- navigation::project_tabs::layout_restore — integration test with a real PostgreSQL WorkbenchLayoutStore mock: switch from project 'a' to 'b', assert layout for 'a' is saved and layout for 'b' (previously stored) is applied to pane state.
egui_kittest visual harness: render ProjectTabBar with 3 projects, assert 3 selectable_label nodes are painted, assert active tab carries selected=true widget info.
AccessKit consumer snapshot (in-process): serialize the UI tree to JSON after render, assert a node with stable_id='project-tab-{id}' exists with role=Button for each project.
implementation_notes: Use ui.push_id(project.id, |ui| { let response = ui.selectable_label(active, &name); ... }) to generate stable AccessKit node ids derived from project.id, not from frame counters.
ProjectTabBar should own a tokio channel receiver for workspace list refresh events; when a message arrives call fetch_workspaces() again.

The selectProject logic in App.tsx (line 1197-1210) resets the entire layout to defaultWorkbenchLayoutState(projectId) on switch; mirror this: load from store, fall back to default, never carry over the old project's open documents.
Keep the tab strip horizontally scrollable (ui.scroll_area or egui ScrollArea wrapping the horizontal layout) if the number of projects exceeds the visible width, with a 24px minimum tab width.
Tab label uses project.name; truncate with ellipsis at 120px if needed (egui::Label::truncate).
Paper-strip aesthetic: slim tab height ~24px, no gap between tabs, underline or background fill for active.
ports_from_react: app/src/App.tsx — ProjectItem type (line 102-105), projects state (line 573), activeProjectId state (line 574), selectProject() function (lines 1197-1210), projects section in left-rail render (lines 1982-2001), listWorkspaces() call (lines 725-742)
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces — returns Workspace[] (api.ts line 733); maps to ProjectItem[] in native shell
GET /workspaces/{id}/workbench/layout — load layout on project switch (api.ts line 1553)
PUT /workspaces/{id}/workbench/layout — save layout before switching away (api.ts line 1559)

risks: Unstable AccessKit node ids (frame-counter derived) break agent targeting after a re-render.
Layout save race: user clicks a new project while the previous layout save is still in flight, overwriting the wrong workspace.
GET /workspaces timeout on startup blocks the entire shell render loop.
Tab strip overflow with many projects causes horizontal clipping with no scroll affordance.
Default layout for a new project is hardcoded and not idiomatic per this project's workspace settings.
minimum_controls: Use ui.push_id(project.id, ...) — never egui auto-id — for every tab button; enforce with a compile-time or test assertion that no tab response uses a frame-derived id.
Layout save must be async and non-blocking; project switch is immediate, save is fire-and-forget with error logging; use MT-006's retry queue.
Workspace list fetch must be spawned to a background async task; shell renders with a loading state or cached list while the fetch is in flight.
Wrap the tab horizontal layout in an egui ScrollArea with show_horizontal_scrollbar=true when rendered tabs exceed available width.
Default layout fallback must call the same defaultWorkbenchLayoutState(project_id) factory used by MT-006; not an ad-hoc inline struct.

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independently confirmed PASS: the top project-tab strip honestly implements project-only switching with a real save-old/load-new layout restore through the reused MT-009 PostgreSQL-backed persistence path, stable AccessKit Tab/TabList ids proven in the live tree, no stubs/ignores on the required path, all 7 acceptance criteria met by substantive end-to-end tests.
code_examined: D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake Worktrees/wtc-worksurface-shell-v1/src/frontend/handshake_native/src/app.rs (switch_project/save_layout_now/reset_to_default_layout/drive_layout_persistence/load_layout/poll_workspaces, constructor fetch spawn at 677, project-tabs panel wiring at 4024)
D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake Worktrees/wtc-worksurface-shell-v1/src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs (WorkbenchLayoutClient real reqwest GET/PUT layout transport)

acceptance_summary.criterion: Horizontal project-tab strip renders at top above pane grid, one tab per /workspaces result.
acceptance_summary.evidence: project_tabs.rs show() (line 198) paints a horizontal ScrollArea of render_tab() per project; app.rs:4024 wires it into TopBottomPanel::top("handshake_project_tabs").exact_height(PROJECT_TAB_BAR_HEIGHT) between the title bar and the central pane grid; default_project_tabs (app.rs:563) seeds one tab before the spawned GET /workspaces resolves; test live_tree_has_three_project_tabs_with_stable_ids_and_active_selected asserts exactly 3 painted Tab nodes.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Clicking a non-active tab switches active_project_id, saves previous project layout, restores new project layout (or default).
acceptance_summary.evidence: app.rs switch_project() (line 3280) calls save_layout_now() (keyed on the CURRENT active id) at line 3285 BEFORE reset_to_default_layout() at line 3289; drive_layout_persistence (line 3547) loads the stored layout on the next frame when loaded_project_id != active_project_id. test switching_project_saves_old_and_loads_new_layout asserts a's layout (0.41/0.59) saved to the store and b's stored layout (0.2/0.9) applied; switching_to_unstored_project_uses_default_layout asserts SplitWeights::default() fallback for an unstored project, never the old project's layout.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Switching back to the previous project restores the layout saved when leaving it.
acceptance_summary.evidence: Same test, lines 174-181: after a->b, switch b->a and asserts split_weights == a_weights{0.41,0.59} (the layout saved when leaving a). Real round-trip through the in-memory LayoutTransport (the same MT-009 injection seam, set via set_layout_manager).

render_tab (project_tabs.rs:291-312) paints colors.active_bg and a bottom accent underline when is_active; the AccessKit node calls node.set_selected(true) for the active tab (line 330); live_tree test asserts active tab selected=true and inactive tabs selected=false.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Every tab has stable AccessKit author_id from project.id, role=Button/Tab, with Click/Toggle action.
acceptance_summary.evidence: project_tab_egui_id derives egui::Id from the author_id STRING project-tab-{id} (project_tabs.rs:95-97), not frame counters; ui.interact at that fixed id with Sense::click() gives Action::Click/Focus; node.set_role(Role::Tab) + set_author_id(project-tab-{id}) (lines 326-333). Container project-tabs (id 50, Role::TabList) declared in accessibility/registry.rs DECLARED_IDENTITIES. Proven in the LIVE AccessKit tree by live_author_nodes assertions and the honest 21->23 snapshot bump in test_accesskit_ids.rs.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Empty workspace list shows a single disabled 'No projects' tab without crashing.
acceptance_summary.evidence: show() calls render_placeholder when projects.is_empty() (line 212-213); render_placeholder (line 356) emits a NON-clickable Role::Tab with set_disabled() and author_id project-tab-none (Sense::hover only, so it does not trip the interactive-naming gate). test empty_workspace_list_shows_disabled_placeholder_without_crashing asserts the placeholder node exists, no panic, no real project tabs.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Backend unreachable at startup retains previous list and shows inline error label.
acceptance_summary.evidence: apply_fetch_error (project_tabs.rs:180) sets FetchState::Error and does NOT touch self.projects; show() renders an inline colored_label when FetchState::Error (line 225-228). Production fetch_workspaces is spawned non-blocking via rt.spawn (app.rs:677) and polled by poll_workspaces (non-blocking is_finished check), so a 5s reqwest timeout never stalls the render loop. test fetch_error_retains_previous_project_list asserts both tabs retained + Error state + both nodes still in the live tree.

spec_realism.detail: Sub-rule 1 (deferred-live escape): grep of project_tabs.rs and test_project_tabs.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to'/'pending MT-' returned NO matches on the required-behavior path; no #[ignore] tests in either file. Sub-rule 2 (real-resource proof): the real PostgreSQL-backed layout store is REUSED, not rebuilt, exactly as the MT directs ('reuse MT-006 store', 'Do NOT rebuild backend logic'). The production WorkbenchLayoutClient (backend_client.rs:955) maps save/load to real reqwest PUT/GET /workspaces/:id/workbench/layout with timeouts; fetch_workspaces (project_tabs.rs:414) issues a real reqwest GET /workspaces (5s timeout) deserialized defensively via serde_json::Value, and is actually spawned in the production constructor (app.rs:677). The switch round-trip test injects the SAME in-memory LayoutTransport seam the MT-009 tests use (set via set_layout_manager) -- a contract-sanctioned isolation point, not a mock authored to dodge the real store contract. The test constructor's workspaces_handle: None is correct headless isolation, not an escape. Sub-rule 3 (self-cert): satisfied by this independent review per operator directive. The layout-restore state-recovery area (HIGH-priority) is real: save-before-switch ordering and load-on-next-frame are proven by the save-old / load-new / switch-back assertions through the real persistence manager.

concerns_non_blocking: fetch_workspaces() JSON->ProjectItem mapping (project_tabs.rs:414-448) has no dedicated unit test (implementer self-noted MINOR). The mapping is defensive (as_array guard returns Parse error, filter_map skips id-less rows, unwrap_or(id) for missing name) and is exercised indirectly via the apply_fetched/empty/error sinks it feeds. Low-cost follow-up: feed a serde_json array (incl. a malformed row) and assert the ProjectItem vector.

Contract 'EXACT FILES TO CREATE' (src/frontend/navigation/project_tabs.rs, shell.rs, state/app_state.rs) differs from the actual crate layout (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/project_tabs.rs and app.rs). This matches the established sibling-MT layout (tab_bar.rs, split_layout.rs, app.rs) rather than the contract's assumed navigation/ module; it is a paperwork/path-shape mismatch, not a code defect, and per operator directive is NOT a FAIL.

apply_fetched on an empty list does not first()-fallback the active_id (project_tabs.rs:167-176 only falls back when active is absent AND the list is non-empty); poll_workspaces then copies that stale id to active_project_id. Harmless today (placeholder renders, no crash, no switch happens) but worth a note for the future workspace-sidebar refresh MT.

MT contract lifecycle.status is still PENDING and validator_verdict PENDING (paperwork only); per operator directive this independent review IS the certification and PENDING paperwork is not a code FAIL.
